free_text: Excitation functions of 85Rb(p,xn)85m,g,83,82,81Sr
reactions up to 100 MeV: integral tests of cross
section data, comparison of production routes of 83Sr
and thick target yield of 82Sr
Kernforschungsanlage Juelich, Germany
Radionuclide Production Group, NAC, Faure,SA
Cv 28 Cyclotron, Juelich, Germany
Injector cyclotron of COSY, Juelich
The Injector at the PSI
The Separated Sector Cyclotron at NAC
Targets for cross section measurements were prepared by
sedimentation of enriched 85RbCl (99.4%) fine powder
onto 25 micron Cu backing and was covered by 0.5mg of
plastic foil and 10 micron Al foil. Sample thickness
was 5-10 mg/cm2.
For yield measurement about 5 MeV thick pellets were
pressed from enriched 85RbCl. Also enriched 82Kr gas
(90%) was used to measure the yield of (3He,2n)83Sr
process.
Several stacks were
irradiated with 70-100nA beam at 12, 16, 20, 21, 45,
71 and 100MeV primary particle energy for 30 min for
the short half life products and for several hours for
the long half life products. Al, Ti and Cu foils were
used to monitor the beam parameters. No numerical
monitor values were given in the article. The beam
current was also monitored by Faraday cup.
Chemical separation was applied due to strong
matrix activity.
The energy degradation was calculated by the method of
Williamson (1966). Decay data were taken from Browne
and Firestone (1986) (see REL-REF).
HpGe high purity germanium gamma detector
high purity germanium detector for low energy
gamma and X-ray.
decay data were
from Table of radioactive isotopes,p.123-3, Wiley,
New York 1986
- used for
calculation energy degradation
Main contributing error components are:
uncertainty in target thickness           2%
uncertainty in target inhomogeneity       5%
uncertainty in beam intensity   8-11%
uncertainty in detector efficiency        5%
uncertainty in peak area          2-7%
uncertainty in chemical yield             3%
TS
SD: REACTION code updated according to new
EXFOR rules in Subents 006-007.  REL-REF updated. Small
corrections in all Subents. ERR-ANALYS updated.
On. Definition of EN-ERR and ERR-T moved to 002-004.
VS. SF3=N corrected in 002.1 and 002.2.
The uncertainty of the primary energy was
+-0.2 MeV as determined by using the cyclotron
parameters and increased up to 1.0MeV along the stacks.
Total error of the data were up to 15%.
